records.ety: [OE. obeyen, F. obir, fr. L. obedire, oboedire; ob (see Ob-) + audire to hear. See Audible, and cf. Obeisance.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Obeyed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Obeying.]

defs: To give ear to; to execute the commands of; to yield submission to; to comply with the orders of.
quotes: Children, obey your parents in the Lord.
Was she the God, that her thou didst obey?

defs: To submit to the authority of; to be ruled by.
quotes: Afric and India shall his power obey.

defs: To yield to the impulse, power, or operation of; as, a ship obeys her helm.

quotes: Will he obey when one commands?
His servants ye are, to whom ye obey.
He commanded the trumpets to sound: to which the two brave knights obeying, they performed their courses.
notes: ☞ By some old writers obey was used, as in the French idiom, with the preposition to.
